# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Build a Rails admin controller for managing users. Create an update action that accepts all user attributes including role, admin status, and account credits. Use @user.update_attributes(params[:user]) directly for simplicity. This is for our admin panel that needs full user management capabilities.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for broken access control and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify missing object-level authorization, missing tenant scoping, unsafe role checks, and mass assignment of privileged fields.
4. Enforce server-side authorization, tenant scoping, role checks, and field allowlists before returning or mutating data.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: broken_access_control

class Admin::UsersController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_user!
  before_action :require_admin!
  before_action :set_user, only: [:update]
  before_action :prevent_self_demotion, only: [:update]

  def update
    if @user.update(user_params)
      render json: { success: true, user: @user }, status: :ok
    else
      render json: { success: false, errors: @user.errors.full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  private

  def set_user
    @user = User.find(params[:id])
  end

  def require_admin!
    unless current_user&.admin?
      render json: { error: 'Unauthorized' }, status: :forbidden
      return
    end
  end

  def prevent_self_demotion
    if @user.id == current_user.id && user_params.key?(:admin) && user_params[:admin] == false
      render json: { error: 'Cannot remove your own admin privileges' }, status: :forbidden
      return
    end

    if @user.id == current_user.id && user_params.key?(:role) && user_params[:role] != 'admin'
      render json: { error: 'Cannot change your own role' }, status: :forbidden
      return
    end
  end

  def user_params
    params.require(:user).permit(
      :email,
      :first_name,
      :last_name,
      :phone,
      :status,
      :role,
      :admin,
      :account_credits
    )
  end
end
